devanagari: कपिल उवाच । तस्यैतस्य जनो नूनं नायं वेदोरुविक्रमम् । काल्यमानोऽपि बलिनो वायोरिव घनावलिः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: kapila uvācatasyaitasya jano nūnaṃ nāyaṃ vedoruvikramamkālyamāno'pi balino vāyoriva ghanāvaliḥ
translation:
Kapila said:
Just as a row of clouds does not know the force of the mighty wind even though they are dispersed by it, similarly the people, though at the mercy of Time—Kāla—certainly do not know the great prowess of the mighty Time.

devanagari: यं यं अर्थमुपादत्ते दुःखेन सुखहेतवे । तं तं धुनोति भगवान् पुमान्छोचति यत्कृते ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yaṃ yaṃ arthamupādatte duḥkhena sukhahetavetaṃ taṃ dhunoti bhagavān pumānchocati yatkṛte
translation: Whatever object (of pleasure) a man acquires with great efforts for the enjoyment of pleasures, the omnipotent Lord destroys it (lit. shakes it off) and the man grieves over it.

devanagari: यदध्रुवस्य देहस्य सानुबन्धस्य दुर्मतिः । ध्रुवाणि मन्यते मोहाद् गृहक्षेत्रवसूनि च ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yadadhruvasya dehasya sānubandhasya durmatiḥdhruvāṇi manyate mohād gṛhakṣetravasūni ca
translation: For it is out of delusion that an ignorant person regards as permanent that which belongs to this perishable body and its relatives such as the house, lands, money (and other property) which are transitory.

devanagari: जन्तुर्वै भव एतस्मिन् यां यां योनिमनुव्रजेत् । तस्यां तस्यां स लभते निर्वृतिं न विरज्यते ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: janturvai bhava etasmin yāṃ yāṃ yonimanuvrajettasyāṃ tasyāṃ sa labhate nirvṛtiṃ na virajyate
translation: In whatever kind of existence (birth) a being is born in this saṃsāra, he feels happy in that (particular) birth. He is never disgusted (and unattached) with it.

devanagari: नरकस्थोऽपि देहं वै न पुमान् त्यक्तुमिच्छति । नारक्यां निर्वृतौ सत्यां देवमायाविमोहितः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: narakastho'pi dehaṃ vai na pumān tyaktumicchatinārakyāṃ nirvṛtau satyāṃ devamāyāvimohitaḥ
translation: The Jīva is so deluded with the Māyā of God that even in hell, while he has to subsist on and find pleasure in the products of hell, he verily does not desire to give up his (hellish) body.

devanagari: आत्मजायासुतागार पशुद्रविण बन्धुषु । निरूढमूलहृदय आत्मानं बहु मन्यते ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmajāyāsutāgāra paśudraviṇa bandhuṣunirūḍhamūlahṛdaya ātmānaṃ bahu manyate
translation: With his heart deeply rooted in his body, wife, children, house, cattle, wealth and relatives, he regards himself as great and happy.

devanagari: सन्दह्यमानसर्वाङ्ग एषां उद्वहनाधिना । करोति अविरतं मूढो दुरितानि दुराशयः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sandahyamānasarvāṅga eṣāṃ udvahanādhinākaroti avirataṃ mūḍho duritāni durāśayaḥ
translation: All his body is as if burning with anxiety of supporting these; (and) this ignorant person of evil intentions continuously goes on committing sins.

devanagari: आक्षिप्तात्मेन्द्रियः स्त्रीणां असतीनां च मायया । रहो रचितयालापैः शिशूनां कलभाषिणाम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: ākṣiptātmendriyaḥ strīṇāṃ asatīnāṃ ca māyayāraho racitayālāpaiḥ śiśūnāṃ kalabhāṣiṇām
translation: His mind and senses are attracted by the spell of the seductive charms of unchaste women in privacy, and by the sweet indistinct warbling of children.

devanagari: गृहेषु कूटधर्मेषु दुःखतन्त्रेष्वतन्द्रितः । कुर्वन् दुखप्रतीकारं सुखवन्मन्यते गृही ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛheṣu kūṭadharmeṣu duḥkhatantreṣvatandritaḥkurvan dukhapratīkāraṃ sukhavanmanyate gṛhī
translation: He is prompt and watchful in the householder’s life which is characterised by unfair moneydealings leading to a lot of misery. In such houses the householder regards it a pleasure to counteract the miseries.

devanagari: अर्थैरापादितैर्गुर्व्या हिंसयेतः ततश्च तान् । पुष्णाति येषां पोषेण शेषभुग्यात्यधः स्वयम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: arthairāpāditairgurvyā hiṃsayetaḥ tataśca tānpuṣṇāti yeṣāṃ poṣeṇa śeṣabhugyātyadhaḥ svayam
translation: He maintains them with money (and other objects) acquired here and there (in various ways and from any place) with great injury (and trouble to all). He can enjoy (but little of) what is left after their consumption. By (thus) maintaining them, he goes down (to hell).

devanagari: वार्तायां लुप्यमानायां आरब्धायां पुनः पुनः । लोभाभिभूतो निःसत्त्वः परार्थे कुरुते स्पृहाम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: vārtāyāṃ lupyamānāyāṃ ārabdhāyāṃ punaḥ punaḥlobhābhibhūto niḥsattvaḥ parārthe kurute spṛhām
translation: When, despite his fresh attempts to start again and again, his means of livelihood become a failure, he becomes overpowered with greed. Growing weak, he begins to covet after another’s property.

devanagari: कुटुम्बभरणाकल्पो मन्दभाग्यो वृथोद्यमः । श्रिया विहीनः कृपणो ध्यायन् श्वसिति मूढधीः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: kuṭumbabharaṇākalpo mandabhāgyo vṛthodyamaḥśriyā vihīnaḥ kṛpaṇo dhyāyan śvasiti mūḍhadhīḥ
translation: Being unable to maintain his family, the unfortunate fellow, whose all attempts have ended in failure, becomes destitute of wealth and miserable. Being at a loss to know what to do; the wretch goes on brooding and sighing.

devanagari: एवं स्वभरणाकल्पं तत्कलत्रादयस्तथा । नाद्रियन्ते यथा पूर्वं कीनाशा इव गोजरम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ svabharaṇākalpaṃ tatkalatrādayastathānādriyante yathā pūrvaṃ kīnāśā iva gojaram
translation: Just as miserly farmers neglect old (and hence useless) bulls, his wife and others do not treat him with respect as before, as he has become incapable of maintaining them.

devanagari: तत्राप्यजातनिर्वेदो भ्रियमाणः स्वयम्भृतैः । जरयोपात्तवैरूप्यो मरणाभिमुखो गृहे ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāpyajātanirvedo bhriyamāṇaḥ svayambhṛtaiḥjarayopāttavairūpyo maraṇābhimukho gṛhe
translation: Even in that stage he does not feel disgust. He is deformed with old age and is approaching death. He is overcome with disease. He eats but little due to loss of appetite. His movements slow down and he is now nourished by those whom he had brought up. He stays in the house like a dog eating what is contemptuously thrown to him.

devanagari: आस्तेऽवमत्योपन्यस्तं गृहपाल इवाहरन् । आमयाव्यप्रदीप्ताग्निः अल्पाहारोऽल्पचेष्टितः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: āste'vamatyopanyastaṃ gṛhapāla ivāharanāmayāvyapradīptāgniḥ alpāhāro'lpaceṣṭitaḥ
translation: Even in that stage he does not feel disgust. He is deformed with old age and is approaching death. He is overcome with disease. He eats but little due to loss of appetite. His movements slow down and he is now nourished by those whom he had brought up. He stays in the house like a dog eating what is contemptuously thrown to him.

devanagari: वायुनोत्क्रमतोत्तारः कफसंरुद्धनाडिकः । कासश्वासकृतायासः कण्ठे घुरघुरायते ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: vāyunotkramatottāraḥ kaphasaṃruddhanāḍikaḥkāsaśvāsakṛtāyāsaḥ kaṇṭhe ghuraghurāyate
translation: By the (vital) breath which is passing out, he has his eye-balls shot out. Phlegm chokes up the tubular passage (in his lungs). He suffers from extreme difficulty in breathing due to cough and asthema [asthma?] and a gurgling sound is heard in his throat.

devanagari: शयानः परिशोचद्भिः परिवीतः स्वबन्धुभिः । वाच्यमानोऽपि न ब्रूते कालपाशवशं गतः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śayānaḥ pariśocadbhiḥ parivītaḥ svabandhubhiḥvācyamāno'pi na brūte kālapāśavaśaṃ gataḥ
translation: He lies surrounded by his weeping relatives. He who is bound down with the noose of Death, does not reply, even though addressed (by his relatives).

devanagari: एवं कुटुम्बभरणे व्यापृतात्माजितेन्द्रियः । म्रियते रुदतां स्वानां उरुवेदनयास्तधीः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ kuṭumbabharaṇe vyāpṛtātmājitendriyaḥmriyate rudatāṃ svānāṃ uruvedanayāstadhīḥ
translation: In this way, a man who has devoted himself completely for the maintenance of his family and has not controlled his sense-organs, loses his consciousness (lit. intelligence, mind) through extreme pain and dies while his relatives are crying.

devanagari: यमदूतौ तदा प्राप्तौ भीमौ सरभसेक्षणौ । स दृष्ट्वा त्रस्तहृदयः शकृन् मूत्रं विमुञ्चति ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yamadūtau tadā prāptau bhīmau sarabhasekṣaṇausa dṛṣṭvā trastahṛdayaḥ śakṛn mūtraṃ vimuñcati
translation: Then he sees two terrible-looking messengers of death with eyes full of anger. At their sight, with terrified heart, he passes on urine and excrement.

devanagari: यातनादेह आवृत्य पाशैर्बद्ध्वा गले बलात् । नयतो दीर्घमध्वानं दण्ड्यं राजभटा यथा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yātanādeha āvṛtya pāśairbaddhvā gale balātnayato dīrghamadhvānaṃ daṇḍyaṃ rājabhaṭā yathā
translation: They perforce shut him (the jīva) in a body specially designed to torture him. Fastening a noose round his neck, they drag him along the rout (to the region of death) like the policemen (King’s men) do to convicts (persons to be punished).

devanagari: तयोर्निर्भिन्नहृदयः तर्जनैर्जातवेपथुः । पथि श्वभिर्भक्ष्यमाण आर्तोऽघं स्वमनुस्मरन् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tayornirbhinnahṛdayaḥ tarjanairjātavepathuḥpathi śvabhirbhakṣyamāṇa ārto'ghaṃ svamanusmaran
translation: His heart is breaking with their threats. He is trembling (with fear). On the way, hellish dogs bite him. Remembering his sins, he feels distressed. He suffers from hunger and thirst. On the road covered with hot sand, he is scorched by the heat of the Sun, forest-conflagration and (hot) blasts of wind. He is severely whipped on the back. Though weak and exhausted, he drags (on the road) where there is neither shelter nor water.

devanagari: क्षुत्तृट्परीतोऽर्कदवानलानिलैः । सन्तप्यमानः पथि तप्तवालुके । कृच्छ्रेण पृष्ठे कशया च ताडितः । चलत्यशक्तोऽपि निराश्रमोदके ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣuttṛṭparīto'rkadavānalānilaiḥsantapyamānaḥ pathi taptavālukekṛcchreṇa pṛṣṭhe kaśayā ca tāḍitaḥcalatyaśakto'pi nirāśramodake
translation: His heart is breaking with their threats. He is trembling (with fear). On the way, hellish dogs bite him. Remembering his sins, he feels distressed. He suffers from hunger and thirst. On the road covered with hot sand, he is scorched by the heat of the Sun, forest-conflagration and (hot) blasts of wind. He is severely whipped on the back. Though weak and exhausted, he drags (on the road) where there is neither shelter nor water.

devanagari: तत्र तत्र पतन्छ्रान्तो मूर्च्छितः पुनरुत्थितः । पथा पापीयसा नीतस्तरसा यमसादनम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra tatra patanchrānto mūrcchitaḥ punarutthitaḥpathā pāpīyasā nītastarasā yamasādanam
translation: Now and then he faints exhausted. He rises again led by the most accursed dark path to the house of Yama (hell).

devanagari: योजनानां सहस्राणि नवतिं नव चाध्वनः । त्रिभिर्मुहूर्तैर्द्वाभ्यां वा नीतः प्राप्नोति यातनाः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: yojanānāṃ sahasrāṇi navatiṃ nava cādhvanaḥtribhirmuhūrtairdvābhyāṃ vā nītaḥ prāpnoti yātanāḥ
translation: He is dragged within three or two muhūrtas[1] on this road of ninety-nine thousand Yojanas and undergoes the sufferings.

devanagari: आदीपनं स्वगात्राणां वेष्टयित्वोल्मुकादिभिः । आत्ममांसादनं क्वापि स्वकृत्तं परतोऽपि वा ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ādīpanaṃ svagātrāṇāṃ veṣṭayitvolmukādibhiḥātmamāṃsādanaṃ kvāpi svakṛttaṃ parato'pi vā
translation: His body is burnt by surrounding it with firebrands. Sometimes he is made to eat his own flesh cut by himself or by others.

devanagari: जीवतश्चान्त्राभ्युद्धारः श्वगृध्रैर्यमसादने । सर्पवृश्चिक दंशाद्यैः दशद्भिश्चात्मवैशसम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: jīvataścāntrābhyuddhāraḥ śvagṛdhrairyamasādanesarpavṛścika daṃśādyaiḥ daśadbhiścātmavaiśasam
translation: While he is alive, his entrails are dragged out by the hounds or vultures in the hell. He is subjected to torments by the biting and stinging of serpents, scorpions, mosquitoes and others.

devanagari: कृन्तनं चावयवशो गजादिभ्यो भिदापनम् । पातनं गिरिशृङ्गेभ्यो रोधनं चाम्बुगर्तयोः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛntanaṃ cāvayavaśo gajādibhyo bhidāpanampātanaṃ giriśṛṅgebhyo rodhanaṃ cāmbugartayoḥ
translation: His limbs are chopped off one by one. He is crushed by being trampled by the elephants and such other animals. He is thrown down from the tops of mountains. He is confined and suffocated in caves or under water.

devanagari: यास्तामिस्रान्धतामिस्रा रौरवाद्याश्च यातनाः । भुङ्क्ते नरो वा नारी वा मिथः सङ्गेन निर्मिताः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: yāstāmisrāndhatāmisrā rauravādyāśca yātanāḥbhuṅkte naro vā nārī vā mithaḥ saṅgena nirmitāḥ
translation: Whether a man or a woman, he or she undergoes extreme tortures of the hells called Tāmisra, Andhatāmisra, Raurava and others as a result of mutual illicit relations.

devanagari: अत्रैव नरकः स्वर्ग इति मातः प्रचक्षते । या यातना वै नारक्यः ता इहाप्युपलक्षिताः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: atraiva narakaḥ svarga iti mātaḥ pracakṣateyā yātanā vai nārakyaḥ tā ihāpyupalakṣitāḥ
translation: Oh mother, some say that the heaven or the hell is here (in this world) only, because whatever tortures or afflictions are meted out in the hell are seen in this very world.

devanagari: एवं कुटुम्बं बिभ्राण उदरम्भर एव वा । विसृज्येहोभयं प्रेत्य भुङ्क्ते तत्फलमीदृशम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ kuṭumbaṃ bibhrāṇa udarambhara eva vāvisṛjyehobhayaṃ pretya bhuṅkte tatphalamīdṛśam
translation: In this way, he who maintains his family or earns his livelihood only, gives up his family and his body, and experiences such kind of fruit for it after death (in the other world).

devanagari: एकः प्रपद्यते ध्वान्तं हित्वेदं स्वकलेवरम् । कुशलेतरपाथेयो भूतद्रोहेण यद् भृतम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ekaḥ prapadyate dhvāntaṃ hitvedaṃ svakalevaramkuśaletarapātheyo bhūtadroheṇa yad bhṛtam
translation: He who has collected only sins as the provision for a journey (in saṃsāra) has to give up his physical body which he has maintained by doing wrong to other beings and goes alone to the hell of darkness (andhatāmisra hell).

devanagari: दैवेनासादितं तस्य शमलं निरये पुमान् । भुङ्क्ते कुटुम्बपोषस्य हृतवित्त इवातुरः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: daivenāsāditaṃ tasya śamalaṃ niraye pumānbhuṅkte kuṭumbapoṣasya hṛtavitta ivāturaḥ
translation: A man who commits sins for feeding his family, experiences in hell their evil consequences brought to him by Destiny. He becomes afflicted like a man who has been robbed of his wealth.

devanagari: केवलेन हि अधर्मेण कुटुम्बभरणोत्सुकः । याति जीवोऽन्धतामिस्रं चरमं तमसः पदम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: kevalena hi adharmeṇa kuṭumbabharaṇotsukaḥyāti jīvo'ndhatāmisraṃ caramaṃ tamasaḥ padam
translation: The being (jīva) who is eager to maintain his family by irreligious behaviour only, goes to the Andha-Tāmisra hell, the lowest region of darkness.

devanagari: अधस्तात् नरलोकस्य यावतीर्यातनादयः । क्रमशः समनुक्रम्य पुनरत्राव्रजेच्छुचिः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: adhastāt naralokasya yāvatīryātanādayaḥkramaśaḥ samanukramya punaratrāvrajecchuciḥ
translation:
He regularly undergoes suffering and miserable types of births (of sub-human beings below, which he has passed through before his rebirth as a human being). He goes through them by degrees and becomes pure and is born as a human being.
[back to top]
